text.title: mcp Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for mcp as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Stdio transport (the default) requires a running subprocess per Claude Code session — no connection pooling.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: For high-volume use cases, consider HTTP transport (custom implementation). Each Claude session spawns a separate server process.

failure-mode.description: TypeScript servers built with "type": "module" require Node.js >= 18; older Node versions silently fail.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Pin Node.js version in .nvmrc or engine in package.json. Test with your minimum supported Node version.

failure-mode.description: FastMCP's @mcp.tool decorator doesn't support streaming responses — use the official SDK for streaming.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: For streaming, use official mcp SDK. For non-streaming, FastMCP is simpler and faster to write.

failure-mode.description: If the MCP server process crashes mid-session, tools become unavailable with no clear error message to the agent.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Implement robust error handling in tool implementations. Return errors as text content, not Python exceptions. Monitor process stability.

failure-mode.description: Tool descriptions are the only interface between the model and the server — vague descriptions cause wrong tool invocation.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Write tool descriptions as complete sentences explaining what the tool does, when to use it, and what it returns. The model uses these to decide which tool to call.

failure-mode.description: Large return payloads (> 100KB) from tools inflate the agent's context window significantly.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Truncate large payloads before returning: json.dumps(data)[:5000] is better than sending 1MB to Claude's context.

failure-mode.description: Python SDK async servers don't work with python -m mcp dev if the event loop is already running (Jupyter).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Use fastmcp for Jupyter environments. For async official SDK, ensure the event loop is fresh.

failure-mode.description: An agent building an MCP server cannot use that server in the same session — it must restart Claude Code after claude mcp add.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Plan multi-session workflows when server installation is part of the task. Document that agents must restart or continue with a new session to use newly-installed servers.

failure-mode.description: If a tool returns an empty string or null, some clients interpret it as a server error — always return at least a status message.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Even in success cases, return a plain-text message like "OK" or "No results found" instead of empty/null.

failure-mode.description: Tool names must be globally unique within a server (no namespacing); name collisions with other registered servers produce unexpected routing.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Prefix tool names with a namespace if building a large server with many tools, e.g., "db_query", "db_list_tables".

failure-mode.description: The model cannot see the server's source code — it only sees tool names and descriptions; a poorly named tool with a misleading description will be misused.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Invest in clear, complete tool descriptions. Include examples in the description if the tool has complex behavior.

failure-mode.description: MCP Inspector (npx @modelcontextprotocol/inspector) opens a browser GUI on port 5173 — not usable in headless agent environments.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Use --cli mode or direct stdio testing instead for CI/headless environments. Test in MCP Inspector during development, use CLI mode for CI validation.

failure-mode.description: Building a server with fastmcp and publishing to PyPI means any version bump is immediately pulled by pip install fastmcp-myserver — pin versions in production registrations.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Pin exact versions in claude mcp add commands: claude mcp add my-server -s user -- npx my-mcp-server@1.2.3.
